prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-Ri6fxm.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-Ri6fxm thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. All existing files and their purposes
2. Existing models/interfaces in src/models/
3. Existing services in src/services/
4. Existing adapters in src/adapters/
5. Event system in src/events/
6. Utils (especially database, logger)
7. The tsconfig.json and package.json setup
8. Any existing patterns for dependency injection or service composition

Read key files in full - especially any existing interfaces, services, and the event system. Report back the full content of the most important files.

parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */
parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted
 * via EventBus. NotificationService and other consumers subscribe
 * to these events independently.
 */

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven architecture with pluggable adapters

Commits fully to EventBus as the single pattern for cross-service
communication, eliminating the CallbackRegistry usage that was documented
as inconsistent. Introduces a NotificationAdapter interface so new
delivery channels (email, webhook, SMS) can be added without modifying
the NotificationService.

Key changes:
- Add notification model types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.)
- Add NotificationAdapter interface and EmailNotificationAdapter stub
- Refactor NotificationService to subscribe to all events via EventBus
  and dispatch through registered adapters
- Fix OrderService to emit status-changed events via EventBus
- Update tests to reflect unified event-driven pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..15d6aa7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter — interface that all notification delivery adapters
+ * must implement.
+ *
+ * Constraint: Every notification channel (email, webhook, SMS, in-app, etc.)
+ * MUST have an adapter that implements this interface. Adapters are registered
+ * with the NotificationService, which dispatches payloads to all of them.
+ *
+ * Adapters are responsible for:
+ * - Accepting a NotificationPayload and delivering it via their channel.
+ * - Returning a boolean indicating success (true) or failure (false).
+ * - Handling their own retries/error logging internally.
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles. */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
+   * @returns true if delivery succeeded, false otherwise.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<boolean>;
+}

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ad3fce9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model and related types.
+ *
+ * Architectural Decision: Event-Driven Notification System
+ * =========================================================
+ * Pattern: EventBus with typed subscribers and pluggable adapters.
+ *
+ * How it works:
+ * 1. Domain services (OrderService, UserService) emit events via EventBus.
+ * 2. NotificationService subscribes to relevant events on the EventBus.
+ * 3. When an event arrives, NotificationService builds a NotificationPayload
+ *    and dispatches it through all registered NotificationAdapter instances.
+ * 4. Each adapter (email, webhook, in-app, SMS, etc.) implements the
+ *    NotificationAdapter interface and handles delivery for its channel.
+ *
+ * Why event-driven over direct calls:
+ * - Loose coupling: domain services don't know about notifications.
+ * - Open for extension: add new notification channels by adding adapters,
+ *   not by modifying existing services.
+ * - Testable: EventBus provides an event log; adapters can be stubbed.
+ * - Consistent: one pattern for all cross-service communication.
+ *
+ * Constraints (for all developers building on this foundation):
+ * - All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService.
+ *   Never call an adapter directly from a domain service.
+ * - All delivery adapters MUST implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - All notification types MUST be registered in the NotificationType union.
+ * - NotificationPayload is the canonical shape passed to every adapter.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification types. Extend this union as new notification
+ * triggers are added.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order:confirmation'
+  | 'order:status-update'
+  | 'user:welcome'
+  | 'user:profile-updated';
+
+/**
+ * Supported delivery channels. Each channel maps to a NotificationAdapter.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'in-app' | 'webhook' | 'sms';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ * This is the single contract between NotificationService and all adapters.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification type for routing and templating. */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** Recipient identifier (e.g., email address, user ID). */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Short subject/title for the notification. */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content. */
+  body: string;
+  /** Arbitrary metadata for adapter-specific rendering. */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * A persisted notification record (after delivery attempt).
+ */
+export interface Notification {
+  id: string;
+  type: NotificationType;
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  recipient: string;
+  subject: string;
+  body: string;
+  status: 'sent' | 'failed';
+  sentAt: Date;
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
